observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to obtain  the supporting documents evidencing the ownership of the recorded six  parcels of land totaling ₱2,051,673.31 and facilitate the immediate registration and titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the recognition in the books  of the 54 unrecorded parcels of land once the  TCT’s are issued  under the name of the Municipality on the basis of the assessed /appraised value/s as prescribed in IPSAS 17;  (c) instruct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to to undertake the applicable mode of disposal, proper documentation, adequate publicity and notification for public auction “as is where is” as soon as possible to avoid further deterioration and decline in the  value  of the unserviceable assets; (d) require the General Services Office to conduct the annual physical inventory of its PPE; and (e) require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office to maintain PPELC and PC for each category of PPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accountable Officer to (a) update and balance his cashbook on a daily basis to closely monitor cash advances /balances and payments/liquidations made by reconciling the cash on hand with the cash balance recorded in the cashbook; (b) foot and close the books at the end of each month; and (c) prepare monthly certification in the cashbook based on his total monthly cash accountabilities   and the ending balances at the end of the month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a)
MSWDO and General Service Officer (GSO) to submit the DL of food packs
distributed to Municipality’s constituents; (b) MHO and/or HRMO Officers to
submit a certified list of frontliners and number of patients served with set meals
during the pandemic; (c) procuring offices and the BAC to ensure that the
procurement documents such as PR and PO are complete with information
necessary to justify the reasonableness of cost of items to be purchased.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the (a) Municipal
Accountant (MA) and all Departments/Offices concerned to submit the deficient
documents to confirm the validity of the payments made for hazard pay; and (b)
MA and Municipal Treasurer (MT) to desist from paying hazard pay and other
allowances to officials/employees that are not substantiated with sufficient
documentation.

We reiterated on our previous year’s audit that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to (a) secure an approval, authorization and ratification from the Sangguniang Bayan for the supplemental budget totaling ₱3,854,105.00 which was disbursed without appropriations pursuant to Sections 336 and 337 of RA No. 7160 and Sections  4 and 85 of P.D. No. 1445; and (b) refrain from incurring expenditures for programs, projects and activities beyond their appropriations.

We reiterated on our previous year’s that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the documentary requirements to support the emergency procurement made from various suppliers; (b) the concerned officials to comply with the posting requirements pursuant to GPPB Resolutions No. 18-2020 and to adopt  competitive public bidding in the procurement of goods and services; and (c) submit the report on the distribution list of commodities during the pandemic.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the authorized LGU representative, the Municipal Social Welfare Development (MSWD) Officer and other concerned officials  to (a) impose stricter measures in the profiling and monitoring of payouts to the beneficiaries specifically in times of calamities such as conduct of strict house to house screening and taking photos of the beneficiary during payouts in accordance with the requirements of DSWD MC No. 9 series of 2020; and (b) submit complete  and valid  documentary requirements to support all  fund disbursements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the  (a)concerned department heads /officials  to submit the deficient documents to validate the propriety and validity of the payments made for Hazard Pay; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to desist from the payment of hazard pay and other allowances relative to COVID 19 outbreak which runs counter to the provisions of applicable laws, rules, and regulations and not  supported with proper and adequate documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Service Officer (a) to submit an inventory tracking schedule/report to properly account for all welfare goods procured or received through donations as well as for timing of distribution and recipients; and (b) implement a standardized monitoring system in the various departments handling the inventory to have a real time access to information on stocks availability.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Implementing Official  to conduct strict evaluation process to ensure that only qualified beneficiaries are granted financial assistance; (b)  then Special Disbursing Officer to refund the excess amount of cash advances, if any; and (c) Municipal Accountant to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement voucher/liquidation report before their processing and payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) desist from allowing  the granting of additional cash advances to officials and employees with previous balances and demand for the immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash advances; (b) withhold the salaries or any money due the accountable officers with outstanding cash advances as provided in Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and (c) discontinue the granting of cash advances to the elected officials for purposes other than their official travelling expenses.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disaster and Risk   Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to submit within the prescribed period the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization to the Audit Team for a timely review of the projects and activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to (a) adhere with the guidelines on the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by implementing only the allowable development projects; and (b)   conduct rational assessment of  the possible impact of the projects to be implemented to the  socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU and which  shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the conduct of public bidding, awarding of contracts, and payment of contract price of several infrastructure projects and on the full payment made on the unimplemented projects; and (ii) submit the lacking bidding documents, minutes of the pre-procurement conference, pre-bid conference and receipt and opening of bids, and  the supporting documents to the deficient contract documents to prevent the suspension of the transactions; (b) require the contractor to (i) implement the project which was already paid in full for the contract price; and/or demand the refund of the full amount; and (ii) require the posting of signboard/billboards at all venues where the PPAs will be implemented in accordance with the full disclosure policy of the government; (c) require the BAC to submit the copy of the contract documents to the Audit Team within five (5) days from issuance for auditorial and technical review; and (d) strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations governing the procurement of infrastructure projects pursuant to Revised IRR of R.A. No.  9184, COA Circulars No. 2009-01 and 2012-001, respectively and other related government rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) provide justification in resorting to the alternative method of procurement instead of competitive bidding in the purchase of Information Technology (IT) Equipment; and (ii) to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 regarding procurement of goods through competitive bidding.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive increase the fidelity bonds of the Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer commensurate to their fund accountabilities, in accordance with Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) verify that the amount of remittance to concerned regulatory agencies is equal to the amounts withheld; and (b) remit any verified underpayment to the appropriate agency keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed by other agencies becomes a personal liability of the negligent employee or official concerned.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive fully comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memoranda Circular No. 2012-01 and 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to adhere to the   provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 by submitting to the COA Auditor’s Office at the beginning of the year  the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year. Further, Management should comply with   the regulation for the signboards/billboards to be posted at all venues where the PPAs of the Municipality will be implemented in order to fully adopt the full  disclosure policy of all transactions governing public interest. Lastly, desist from the practice of putting billboards/signboards bearing the name and images of the local official on PPAs.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Local  Chief Executive require (a) direct the BAC, Secretariat and TWG  to (i)provide advance copies of contracts and their corresponding supporting documents to the COA within the prescribed period;(ii) to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and  Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 regarding the conduct of bidding, including the proper mechanism on the use of the PhilGEPs; (ii) adhere strictly to the spirit and intent of the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on achieving transparency and promoting competition to obtain the most advantageous price and terms for the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (i) henceforth, transmit immediately to the Audit Team all unsubmitted vouchers and documents  to prevent the suspension/disallowance of payments made;  (ii) ensure that the submission of accounts is invariably complied within the prescribed period ; and (iii) submit   justifications and the lacking documents/requirements, as stated above, for the evaluation of the   Audit Team.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local  Chief Executive comply with the requirements of Section 4 of AO No. 17, particularly on the procurement of common-use supplies from the  PS-DBM based on the Annual Procurement Plan. Otherwise, obtain certification from the PS-DBM on the non-availability of stocks on the subject items of the procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to (a) secure an approval, authorization and ratification from the Sangguniang Bayan for the supplemental budget totaling ₱3,854,105.00 which was disbursed without appropriations pursuant to Sections 336 and 337 of RA No. 7160 and Sections  4 and 85 of P.D. No. 1445; and (b) refrain from incurring expenditures for programs, projects and activities beyond their appropriations.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the documentary requirements to support the emergency procurement made from various suppliers; (b) the concerned officials to comply with the posting requirements pursuant to GPPB Resolutions No. 18-2020 and to adopt  competitive public bidding in the procurement of goods and services; and (c) submit the report on the distribution list of commodities during the pandemic.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the authorized LGU representative, the Municipal Social Welfare Development (MSWD) Officer and other concerned officials  to (a) impose stricter measures in the profiling and monitoring of payouts to the beneficiaries specifically in times of calamities such as conduct of strict house to house screening and taking photos of the beneficiary during payouts in accordance with the requirements of DSWD MC No. 9 series of 2020; and (b) submit complete  and valid  documentary requirements to support all  fund disbursements.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the  (a)concerned department heads /officials  to submit the deficient documents to validate the propriety and validity of the payments made for Hazard Pay; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to desist from the payment of hazard pay and other allowances relative to COVID 19 outbreak which runs counter to the provisions of applicable laws, rules, and regulations and not  supported with proper and adequate documentation.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Service Officer (a) to submit an inventory tracking schedule/report to properly account for all welfare goods procured or received through donations as well as for timing of distribution and recipients; and (b) implement a standardized monitoring system in the various departments handling the inventory to have a real time access to information on stocks availability.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Implementing Official  to conduct strict evaluation process to ensure that only qualified beneficiaries are granted financial assistance; (b)  then Special Disbursing Officer to refund the excess amount of cash advances, if any; and (c) Municipal Accountant to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement voucher/liquidation report before their processing and payment.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) desist from allowing  the granting of additional cash advances to officials and employees with previous balances and demand for the immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash advances; (b) withhold the salaries or any money due the accountable officers with outstanding cash advances as provided in Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and (c) discontinue the granting of cash advances to the elected officials for purposes other than their official travelling expenses.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to obtain  the supporting documents evidencing the ownership of the recorded six  parcels of land totaling ₱2,051,673.31 and facilitate the immediate registration and titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the recognition in the books  of the 54 unrecorded parcels of land once the  TCT’s are issued  under the name of the Municipality on the basis of the assessed /appraised value/s as prescribed in IPSAS 17;  (c) instruct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to to undertake the applicable mode of disposal, proper documentation, adequate publicity and notification for public auction “as is where is” as soon as possible to avoid further deterioration and decline in the  value  of the unserviceable assets; (d) require the General Services Office to conduct the annual physical inventory of its PPE; and (e) require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office to maintain PPELC and PC for each category of PPE.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disaster and Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to submit within the prescribed period the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization to the Audit Team for a timely review of the projects and activities.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to (a) adhere with the guidelines on the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by implementing only the allowable development projects; and (b)   conduct rational assessment of  the possible impact of the projects to be implemented to the  socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU and which  shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures for the benefit of the constituents.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the conduct of public bidding, awarding of contracts, and payment of contract price of several infrastructure projects and on the full payment made on the unimplemented projects; and (ii) submit the lacking bidding documents, minutes of the pre-procurement conference, pre-bid conference and receipt and opening of bids, and  the supporting documents to the deficient contract documents to prevent the suspension of the transactions; (b) require the contractor to (i) implement the project which was already paid in full for the contract price; and/or demand the refund of the full amount; and (ii) require the posting of signboard/billboards at all venues where the PPAs will be implemented in accordance with the full disclosure policy of the government; (c) require the BAC to submit the copy of the contract documents to the Audit Team within five (5) days from issuance for auditorial and technical review; and (d) strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations governing the procurement of infrastructure projects pursuant to Revised IRR of R.A. No.  9184, COA Circulars No. 2009-01 and 2012-001, respectively and other related government rules and regulations.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) provide justification in resorting to the alternative method of procurement instead of competitive bidding in the purchase of Information Technology (IT) Equipment; and (ii) to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 regarding procurement of goods through competitive bidding.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accountable Officer to (a) update and balance his cashbook on a daily basis to closely monitor cash advances /balances and payments/liquidations made by reconciling the cash on hand with the cash balance recorded in the cashbook; (b) foot and close the books at the end of each month; and (c) prepare monthly certification in the cashbook based on his total monthly cash accountabilities   and the ending balances at the end of the month.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  increase the fidelity bonds of the Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer commensurate to their fund accountabilities, in accordance with Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) verify that the amount of remittance to concerned regulatory agencies is equal to the amounts withheld; and (b) remit any verified underpayment to the appropriate agency keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed by other agencies becomes a personal liability of the negligent employee or official concerned.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive fully comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memoranda Circular No. 2012-01 and 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the   MTO to complete the conduct of the physical inventory and provide in    the RPCPPE the balance on hand,  per card and discrepancies, if any;  (b) require the MTO and the MAO   to maintain property records (i) to facilitate the reconciliation of  records vis-à-vis the RPCPPE and  (ii) for internal control purposes; (c) require the MAO, MTO and the Municipal Assessor to reconcile  their records of the Land account (i) to properly identify the  Municipality-owned real properties not yet titled under its name and  undertake the legal process of title transfer; and (ii) for proper   recording in the books of those lots with TCTs under the name of the Municipality; and (d) create a Disposal Committee that shall be tasked to undertake the applicable mode of disposal, proper documentation, adequate publicity and notification for public auction “as is where is” as soon as possible to avoid further deterioration of the unserviceable assets.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer(a) maintain a cashbook for Petty Cash Fund; (b) update and balance the cashbook dailyto closely monitor cash advances /balance and payments/liquidations made by reconciling the cash on hand with   the cash balance recorded in the cashbook; and (c) foot and close the books at the end of each month and prepare a monthly certification in   the cashbook of the total monthly cash accountabilities. Further, the AO and the Municipal Accountant should reconcile the cashbook and the general /subsidiary ledger at least at the end of each quarter.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to (a) revisit the terms and conditions of the Memorandum of Agreement and make the necessary amendments to ensure compliance with the PPP Law and obtain the most advantageous terms for the government; (b) ensure that public funds are spent in accordance with laws, rules and regulations and exclusively used for the benefit of targeted beneficiaries;   and  (c)address the issues and concerns of the beneficiaries  so that the   intended  objectives of the project may be fully achieved.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local  Chief Executive, as head of the Municipal Development Council:  (a) prioritize the DF on PPAs that   are in the nature of capital investments which contribute to the development agenda of the Municipality, pursuant to the provisions of the JMC; and (b)direct the Municipal Accountant to support all disbursements with complete documentary requirements to determine validity, reliability and/or existence thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to include in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to help support the DRRM activities of the LDRRMC; and (b) continue to comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 on the allocation of LDRRMF.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (a) request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan for the immediate enactment of an Ordinance on mandatory waste segregation at source and no-segregation, no-collection policy:  (b) require the barangay officials through the Liga ng mga Barangay  to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (c) fully implement the SCRP; and  (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the two barangays to establish their own MRF and to encourage the proper usage of the 17 non-functional MRFs.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to(a) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute;          and (b) request the Local School Board to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant,  in coordination with other offices involved, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement vouchers before their processing and payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and  Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 regarding procurement of goods through competitive bidding.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GFPS and other concerned offices to facilitate the establishment of the Municipal’s GAD database pursuant to JMC 2013-01as amended by Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016re: Guidelines on the Localization of    the Magna Carta of Women.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local  Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) exert extra efforts to retrieve the prior years’ records to determine the cause(s) for the noted discrepancies in the inter-agency payables accounts; and (b) cause the remittance of any verified underpayment to agencies concerned keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed becomes a personal liability of the erring accountable officials.

observations.managementAction: Reiterated under Observation No. 13 of CY 2019 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the MTO to complete the conduct of the physical inventory and provide in the RPCPPE the balance on hand,  per card and discrepancies, if any; (b) require the MTO and the MAO to maintain property records (i) to facilitate the reconciliation of records vis-à-vis the RPCPPE and (ii) for internal control purposes; (c) require the MAO, MTO and the Municipal Assessor to reconcile their records of the Land account (i) to properly identify the Municipality-owned real properties not yet titled under its name and  undertake the legal process of title transfer; and (ii) for proper recording in the books of those lots with TCTs under the name of the Municipality; and (d) create a Disposal Committee that shall be tasked to undertake the applicable mode of disposal, proper documentation, adequate publicity and notification for public auction “as is where is” as soon as possible to avoid further deterioration of the unserviceable assets.
Management commented that it will abide by the recommendations.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer (a) maintain a cashbook for Petty Cash Fund; (b) update and balance the cashbook daily to closely monitor cash advances /balance and payments/liquidations made by reconciling the cash on hand with the cash balance recorded in the cashbook; and (c) foot and close the books at the end of each month and prepare a monthly certification in the cashbook of the total monthly cash accountabilities. Further, the AO and the Municipal Accountant should reconcile the cashbook and the general / subsidiary ledger at least at the end of each quarter.
Management promised that it will comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 by submitting to the COA Auditor’s Office at the beginning of the year the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year. Further, Management should comply with the regulation for the signboards/billboards to be posted at all venues where the PPAs of the Municipality will be implemented in order to fully adopt the full disclosure policy of all transactions governing public interest. Lastly, desist from the practice of putting billboards/signboards bearing the name and images of the local official on PPAs.
Management commented that it will abide by the recommendations.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to (a) revisit the terms and conditions of the Memorandum of Agreement and make the necessary amendments to ensure compliance with the PPP Law and obtain the most advantageous terms for the government; (b) ensure that public funds are spent in accordance with laws,    rules and regulations and exclusively used for the benefit of targeted beneficiaries;             and  (c) address the  issues and concerns  of the beneficiaries  so that the intended  objectives of the project may be fully achieved.
Management is persistently coordinating with HWRI to address the issues and concerns of its constituents. The LCE is also considering to terminate the MOA and   operate the Salintubig projects on their own.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive, as head of the Municipal Development Council: (a) prioritize the DF on PPAs that are in the nature of capital investments which contribute to the development agenda of the Municipality, pursuant to the provisions of the JMC; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to support all disbursements with complete documentary requirements to determine validity, reliability and/or existence thereof.
Management commented that it will abide by the recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to include in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to help support the DRRM activities of the LDRRMC; and (b) continue to comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 on the allocation of LDRRMF.
Management commented that it will continue to comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (a) request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan for the immediate enactment of an Ordinance on mandatory waste segregation at source and no-segregation, no-collection policy: (b) require the barangay officials through the Liga ng mga Barangay to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (c) fully implement the SCRP; and  (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the two barangays to establish their own MRF and to encourage the proper usage of the 17 non-functional MRFs.
The Management committed to comply with our audit recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to (a) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (b) request the Local School Board to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization.
Management commented that it will abide by the recommendations.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require  (a) direct the BAC, Secretariat and TWG  to (i) provide advance copies of contracts and their corresponding supporting documents to the COA within the prescribed period; (ii) to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 regarding the conduct of bidding, including the proper mechanism on the use of the PhilGEPs; (ii) adhere strictly to the spirit and intent of the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on achieving transparency and promoting competition to obtain the most advantageous price and terms for the Municipality; and  (b) Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (i) henceforth, transmit immediately to the Audit Team all unsubmitted vouchers and documents  to prevent the suspension/disallowance of payments made;  (ii) ensure that the submission of accounts is invariably complied within the prescribed period ; and (iii) submit justifications and the lacking documents/requirements, as stated above, for the evaluation of the Audit Team.
Management submitted copies of DVs totaling ₱6,496,025.15 on May 11, 2020. It also commented that it will abide by the recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/ attached to the disbursement vouchers before their processing and payment.
Management committed to submit the deficient documents enumerated above.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive comply with the requirements of Section 4 of AO No. 17, particularly on the procurement of common-use supplies from the PS-DBM based on the Annual Procurement Plan. Otherwise, obtain certification from the PS-DBM on the non-availability of stocks on the subject items of the procurement.
Management commented that it will abide by the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 regarding procurement of goods through competitive bidding.
Management commented that it will abide by the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GFPS and other concerned offices to facilitate the establishment of the Municipal’s GAD database pursuant to JMC 2013-01 as amended by Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 re: Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.
The Management committed to comply with our audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) consider adopting the DSWD rates relative to the grant of financial assistance; (b) request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution establishing a set of specific guidelines, for each type of assistance to rationalize disbursements under the Donation account: and (c) observe strict supervision over the evaluation process to ensure that only qualified beneficiaries are granted assistance.
The Management committed to comply with our audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the officials concerned to make payments directly to supplier and limit payments through reimbursement.
The Management committed to comply with our audit recommendations.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) exert extra efforts to retrieve the prior years’ records to determine the cause(s) for the noted discrepancies in the inter-agency payables accounts; and (b) cause the remittance of any verified underpayment to agencies concerned keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed becomes a personal liability of the erring accountable officials.
Management committed to comply with our audit recommendations.

observations.managementAction: Reiterated under Observation No. 1 of CY 2019 AAR
Reiterated under Observation No. 5 of CY 2019 AAR
Reiterated under Observation No. 3 of CY 2019 AAR
Reiterated under Observation No. 6 of CY 2019 AAR
Reiterated under Observation No. 16 of CY 2019 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) include only the priority development programs, projects and activities in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan specifically the appropriation for the 20% Development Fund and (b) to monitor and evaluate its implementation in accordance with the approved development plan.

We recommended Management (a) prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) presenting the 30%, 70% and list of projects and activities to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years; and (b) submit on the prescribed format and period the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization to the Audit Team for the timely review of the projects and activities.

We recommended that Management exert more effort to comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE (a) require the MTO to complete the conduct of the physical inventory count by providing in the RPCPPE the balance on hand and per card, and discrepancies, if any; (b) require the MTO and the MAO to maintain property records (i) to facilitate the reconciliation of records vis-à-vis the RPCPPE and (ii) for control purposes; and (c) require the MAO, MTO and the Municipal Assessor to reconcile their records of the Land account (i) to properly identify the Municipal-owned real properties not yet titled under its name and  undertake the legal process of title transfer and (ii) for proper recording in the books of those lots with TCTs under the name of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendations that the LCE (a) as head of the Municipal Development Council prioritize the DF on PPAs that are in the nature of capital investment which contribute to the development agenda of the Municipality pursuant to the provisions of the JMC; and (b) require the local officials concerned to monitor the implementation of PPAs to avoid the incurrence of expenditures more than the allotted budget.

3.8	We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Offices concerned to undergo feasibility study and procurement planning on every infrastructure project to ensure the implementation of projects without delay; and (b) continue to observe and comply with the provisions of the MOA for the timely implementation and completion of projects; and (b) require the Offices concerned to closely supervise the completion of the remaining projects without further delay for the early enjoyment of benefits of the targeted beneficiaries.
4.5	We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to comply strictly with the aforementioned regulation for the promotion of transparency and accountability.
6.4	We recommended that the LCE direct the GFPS and other concerned offices to facilitate the establishment of the Municipal’s GAD database pursuant to JMC 2013-01.

7.6.	We recommended that Management require the LDRRMC to (a) prepare the LDRRMFIP annually for the immediate implementation of the programs and activities envisioned to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management; and (b) submit the prescribed format of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization to the AT within the prescribed period for the timely review of the projects and activities implemented.

8.6	We recommended that the LCE (a) request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan for the immediate enactment of an Ordinance on mandatory waste segregation at source and no-segregation, no collection policy: (b) require the barangay officials through the Liga ng mga Barangay to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (c) address the issue on the controlled open dumpsite and include provisions in the SWMP for their closure or eventual phase out in accordance with the guidelines set by the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; and  (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the two barangays to establish their own MRF and to encourage the proper usage of the 17 non-functional MRFs.

9.7	We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to (a) exert extra efforts to retrieve the prior years’ records to determine the cause(s) for the noted discrepancies in the inter-agency payables accounts; and (b) cause the remittance of any verified underpayment to agencies concerned keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed becomes a personal liability of the erring accountable officials.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management (a) conduct a complete physical count annually and submit to the accounting office and to the audit team the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to reconcile it with the recorded values in the books; (b) require the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively, for control purposes; (c) initiate the proper disposal of all unserviceable  properties; (d) require the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Assessor, and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to be able to account for all lots owned by the Municipality to pave the way for the recording of the necessary adjustments in the books; and (e) instruct the municipal officials concerned to exert effort to facilitate the retrieval or reproduction of the titles on all lots owned by the Municipality and verify property without TCTs and Deeds of Sale with the Register of Deeds (RD).

We recommended that the Management continue to being compliant on the provision of funds for the local disaster risk reduction and management fund, in institutionalizing the disaster risk reduction and management plan and on the disclosure requirements on the Notes to FS pursuant to the requirements of RA 10121.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the revision/finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; (b) encourage active participation of barangay officials through the Liga ng mga Barangay to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;  (c) address the issue on the controlled open dumpsites and include in the SWMP provisions for their closure or eventual phase out in accordance with the guidelines set by the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; and  (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the 6 barangays to establish their own MRF.

We recommended that the Local Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement vouchers before their processing and payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Budget Officer to fully allocate 1% of the IRA for PPAs intended for the protection of children’s rights, upliftment of their lives and elimination of juvenile delinquency in the locality as envisioned under RA No. 9344.

We recommended that the Local Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that all documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made in consonance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that (a) Management strictly comply with the prescribed period for submission of advance copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders, pre-repair evaluation report, and their supporting documents pursuant to the above stated provisions of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 95-006; and (b) the General Services Office responsible for accepting deliveries, to notify COA of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for ocular verification of the existence and condition of the supplies, materials and equipment. In addition, copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports should also be submitted within 24 hours upon acceptance as mandated by the regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to post all Notices of Award in the PhilGEPS within the prescribed date/period together with the Notices to Proceed and the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects in conformity with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended Management that the further payments of allowances to national government officials be immediately stopped until the Sangguniang Bayan of Pandi pass a resolution authorizing the grant of and specifying the rates of additional allowances to these officials in accordance with the inherent precondition under Section 3 of Local Budget Circular No. 53.
We recommended that Management direct the BAC to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 regarding procurement of goods through competitive bidding.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant (a) exert extra effort to immediately trace back the source of discrepancies noted under the Inter Agencies Payable; and (b) cause the remittance of any verified underpayment to the appropriate agency keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed by other agencies becomes a personal liability of the negligent employee or official concerned.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management (a) conduct physical count annually and submit to the accounting office and to the audit team the RCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to reconcile it with the recorded values in the books; (b) require the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively, for control purposes; (c) initiate the proper disposal of all unserviceable  properties; d) require the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Assessor, and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to be able to account for all lots owned by the Municipality to pave the way for the recording of the necessary adjustments in the books; and (e) instruct the municipal officials concerned to exert effort to facilitate the retrieval or reproduction of the titles on all lots owned by the Municipality and verify property without TCTs and Deeds of Sale with the Register of Deeds (RD).
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No.1 of 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly comply with the prescribed period for submission of disbursement vouchers, advance copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders, pre-repair evaluation report, and their supporting documents pursuant to the above stated provisions of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 95-006.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 9 of 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: 1.17	We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management (a) conduct a complete physical count of PPE annually and submit to the accounting office and to the audit team the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to reconcile it with the recorded values in the books; (b) require the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively, for control purposes; (c) initiate the proper disposal of all unserviceable  properties; d) require the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Assessor, and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to be able to account for all lots owned by the Municipality to pave the way for the recording of the necessary adjustments in the books; and (e) instruct the municipal officials concerned to exert effort to facilitate the retrieval or reproduction of the titles on all lots owned by the Municipality and verify property without TCTs and Deeds of Sale with the Register of Deeds (RD).

We recommended that the Management continue to being compliant on the provision of funds for the local disaster risk reduction and management fund, in institutionalizing the disaster risk reduction and management plan and on the disclosure requirements on the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to the requirements of RA No. 10121.

3.7	We recommended that Management (a) include only the priority development programs, projects and activities in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) specifically the appropriation for the 20% Development Fund and (b) monitor and evaluate its implementation in accordance with the approved development plan.

4.8	We recommended that Management (a) prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) presenting the 30% Quick Response Fund, 70% Mitigation Fund and list of projects and activities to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years; and (b) submit in the prescribed format and period the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization to the Audit Team for the timely review of the projects and activities.

5.16	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the revision/finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; (b) encourage active participation of barangay officials through the Liga ng mga Barangay to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;  (c) address the issue on the controlled open dumpsites and include in the SWMP provisions their closure or eventual phase out in accordance with the guidelines set by the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; and  (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the six barangays to establish their own MRF.

6.6	We recommended that the Local Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the disbursement vouchers before their processing and payment.

7.8	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Budget Officer to fully allocate 1% of the IRA for PPAs intended for the protection of children’s rights, upliftment of their lives and elimination of juvenile delinquency in the locality as envisioned under RA No. 9344.

8.9	We recommended that the Local Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that all documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made in consonance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.

9.9	We reiterated our recommendation that (a) Management strictly comply with the prescribed period for submission of advance copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders, pre-repair evaluation report, and their supporting documents pursuant to the above stated provisions of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 95-006; and (b) the General Services Office responsible for accepting deliveries, to notify COA of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for ocular verification of the existence and condition of the supplies, materials and equipment. In addition, copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports should also be submitted within 24 hours upon acceptance as mandated by the regulation.

10.4	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to post all Notices of Award in the PhilGEPS within the prescribed date/period together with the Notices to Proceed and the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects in conformity with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

11.7	We recommended that Management desist from making further payments of allowances to national government officials until the Sangguniang Bayan of Pandi pass a resolution authorizing the grant of and specifying the rates of additional allowances to these officials in accordance with the inherent precondition under Section 3 of Local Budget Circular No. 53.
12.5	We recommended that Management direct the BAC to comply strictly with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184 regarding procurement of goods through competitive bidding.

13.6	We recommended that Management exert efforts to comply with the GAD mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

14.4	We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Accountant  (a) exert extra effort to immediately trace back the source of discrepancies noted under the Inter Agencies Payable; and (b) cause the remittance of any verified underpayment to the appropriate agency keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed by other agencies becomes a personal liability of the negligent employee or official concerned.

We recommended that Management effect the increase of the fidelity bonds of the Municipality’s accountable officers commensurate to their accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that Management a) stop designating Job Order personnel as collecting officers since the LGU has no employee-employer relationship with them;   b) provide a safety vault/box in the Market Office; and c) ensure strict adherence that all designated accountable officers/employees for the collections of revenues and receipts and other bondable positions are properly bonded in accordance with law, to safeguard government funds.

We recommended that Management
(a) require the Municipal Budget Office   to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; (b) require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that Management (a) prioritize the budget for 20% IRA on projects that would create greater impact in terms of sustainability; and (b) advise the Local Development Council and the municipal officials concerned to prepare the development plan strictly in accordance with the guidelines of the DILG and carry out the targeted activities within the time frame.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) prepare the LDRRMFIP annually for the immediate implementation of the programs and activities envisioned to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management; and (b) submit Monthly Report of Fund Utilization to the COA Office for the timely review of the projects and activities to be undertaken thereon.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 4 of 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; (b) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;
(c) address the issue on the controlled open dumpsites and include in the SWMP provisions for their closure or eventual phase out in accordance with the guidelines set by the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the 6 barangays to establish their own MRF; and  (e)  recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of R.A. No. 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated  under Observation No. 5 of 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management (a) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (b) request the Local School Board to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management comply with the requirements of Section 4 of AO No. 17 particularly on the procurement of common-use supplies from the PS-DBM based on the Annual Procurement Plan, otherwise, obtain certification from the PS-DBM on the non-availability of stocks on the items subject of the procurement.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant (a) exert extra effort to immediately trace back the source of discrepancies noted under the Inter Agencies Payable; and (b) cause the remittance of any verified underpayment to the appropriate agency keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed by other agencies becomes a personal liability of the negligent employee or official concerned.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management (a) conduct physical count annually and submit to the accounting office and to the audit team the RCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to reconcile it with the recorded values in the books; (b) require the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively, for control purposes; (c) initiate the proper disposal of all unserviceable  properties; d) require the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Assessor, and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to be able to account for all lots owned by the Municipality to pave the way for the recording of the necessary adjustments in the books; and (e) instruct the municipal officials concerned to exert effort to facilitate the retrieval or reproduction of the titles on all lots owned by the Municipality and verify property without TCTs and Deeds of Sale with the Register of Deeds (RD).

We recommended that Management effect the increase of the fidelity bonds of the Municipality’s accountable officers commensurate to their accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that Management a) stop designating Job Order personnel as collecting officers since the LGU has no employee-employer relationship with them;  b) provide a safety vault/box in the Market Office; and c) ensure strict adherence that all designated accountable officers/employees for the collections of revenues and receipts and other bondable positions are properly bonded in accordance with law, to safeguard government funds.

We recommended that Management (a) require the Municipal Budget Office   to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; (b) require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that Management (a) prioritize the budget for 20% IRA on projects that would create greater impact in terms of sustainability; and (b) advise the Local Development Council and the municipal officials concerned to prepare the development plan strictly in accordance with the guidelines of the DILG and carry out the targeted activities within the time frame.

We recommended that Management (a) prepare the LDRRMFIP annually for the immediate implementation of the programs and activities envisioned to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management; and (b) submit Monthly Report of Fund Utilization to the COA Office for the timely review of the projects and activities to be undertaken thereon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; (b) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;  (c) address the issue on the controlled open dumpsites and include in the SWMP provisions for their closure or eventual phase out in accordance with the guidelines set by the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the 6 barangays to establish their own MRF; and (e)  recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of R.A. No. 9003.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management (a) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (b) request the Local School Board to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management comply with the requirements of Section 4 of AO No. 17 particularly on the procurement of common-use supplies from the PS-DBM based on the Annual Procurement Plan, otherwise, obtain certification from the PS-DBM on the non-availability of stocks on the items subject of the procurement.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly comply with the prescribed period for submission of disbursement vouchers, advance copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders, pre-repair evaluation report, and their supporting documents pursuant to the above stated provisions of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 95-006.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant (a) exert extra effort to immediately trace back the source of discrepancies noted under the Inter Agencies Payable; and (b) cause the remittance of any verified underpayment to the appropriate agency keeping in mind that any penalty and other charges imposed by other agencies becomes a personal liability of the negligent employee or official concerned.

We recommended that: (a) the Municipal Treasurer refrain from
recording unreleased checks or
checks still in her custody at the end
of every accounting period as credit
to the cashbook balance so that only
the amount of checks actually issued
are recorded in her cashbook; (b)the Municipal Accountant direct the
personnel concerned to draw JEV
for check disbursement transactions
which are duly covered with DV;
and (c)the Municipal Accountant
direct the personnel concerned to
draw JEV to adjust the unreleased
checks and the cancelled checks to
present fairly the recorded cash in
bank and accounts payable accounts.

We recommended that Management
(a) instruct chiefs of offices to
review closely the itineraries of
travel submitted by their staff/
subordinates and be cautious on
fixing the rates of allowances to be
granted; (b) direct the Accounting
Office to stop allowing travel
expenses which are extremely
excessive and not adequately
documented in accordance with the
rules and regulations of EO 298; and
(c) effect the immediate refund of
the disallowed claims.

We recommended that the Local
Chief Executive (a) require the
Accountant and the Treasurer to stop
the payment of annual dues to
various organizations and
associations not in conformity with
R.A. No. 7160 for lack of legal
basis; (b) advise the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that claims
for the membership, dues, and
contributions be substantiated with
the necessary budget and approved
plans and programs as basis for the
amount to be paid; and (c) advise the
Municipal Treasurer that payments
for the purpose made to duly
recognized leagues and federations
under the law be acknowledged with
the prescribed government
Accountable Form No. 51 or official
receipts duly registered with the
Bureau of Internal Revenue.

We recommended that the
Municipality (a) refrain from
approving/ paying disbursements
without proper/ complete
documentations; (b) submit the
lacking required documents and
attachments on deficient transactions
to the Audit Team for its evaluation;
and (c) strictly comply with Section
4 (j), Volume I of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs.
